quranicRelevance: God sufficing His servants ('kafa billahi'), protecting against harm, and adequacy.

keyDerivations.meaning: To suffice, be enough, protect, or save from

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَكَفَى اللَّهُ المُؤْمِنِينَ القِتَالَ (33:25): And God spared/saved the believers from fighting (sufficed them).
discardedNoise: Grammatical debates on the letter ba prefixed to the agent of kafa.
summaryEn: The root denotes sufficiency and protection, framing God's absolute sufficiency for His servants (Kafa) and His defense of the believers.
summaryAr: يدل هذا الجذر على الكفاية والحفظ، مؤطراً لكفاية الله لعباده (كفى) ودفاعه وحفظه للمؤمنين.
rawText: أَكْفَى [More, and most, satisfying]. (Mgh, in art. جزأ.) اِكْتَفَى بِالشَّىْءِ 8 He was, or became, sufficed by the thing, so as to need nothing more; or content with it. (Msb.) كَفَى 1 He, or it, sufficed, or contented: hence كَفَانِى فُلَانٌ الأَمْرَ Such a one sufficed me, or contented me, in respect of the affair; i. e., by taking upon himself to perform it or accomplish it if good, or to prevent it or avert it if evil: and كَفَانِى شَهِيدًا He sufficed me as, or for, a witness; or he suffices me as, or for, such: and sometimes بِ is redundantly prefixed to its agent, and sometimes to its objective complement; as in exs. cited in art. ب. See Ham, p. 152. كَفَى [It sufficed, or satisfied, or contented,] it stood instead of another thing, or other things; (Msb;) [as also كَفَى عَنْ غَيْرِهِ.] === كَفَى بِنَا for كَفَانَا &c. See W, pp. 5 and 6 === كَفَى, aor. يَكْفِى, inf. n. كِفَايَةٌ, also signifies دَفَعَ and مَنَعَ followed by مِنْ. (Har, p. 43, q. v.) [You say, كَفَاهُ الشَّرَّ He repelled from him evil; and hence, he defended him therefrom; and he freed him therefrom: said of God, and of a man, &c.] كِفَايَةٌ A sufficiency; enough; a thing that suffices, or contents, and enables one to obtain what one seeks. (TA, art. بلغ.) === قَامَ بِكِفَايَتِهِ He undertook his maintenance; he maintained him; syn. مَانَهُ. (S, K, art. مون.)
